perturbation.original_text: "Business" means an online real estate media platform in the PRC that (i) provides information and updates related to real estate, home furnishing and construction in the PRC and provides real estate, home furnishing and construction advertising services, and (ii) operates a business-to- business and business-to-consumer Internet platform targeting participants in the PRC real estate industry, in each case, as currently conducted or contemplated to be conducted on the websites owned or operated by Licensee or any of Licensee's Affiliates in the PRC.
perturbation.changed_text: "Business" means an online real estate media platform in the PRC that (i) provides information and updates related to real estate, home furnishing and construction in the PRC and provides real estate, home furnishing and construction advertising services, and (ii) operates a business-to- business and business-to-consumer Internet platform targeting participants in the PRC real estate industry, in each case, as currently conducted or contemplated to be conducted on the websites owned or operated by Licensor or any of Licensor's Affiliates in the PRC.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Business' originally specifies it is conducted on websites owned by the Licensee or Licensee's affiliates. By changing this to Licensor or Licensor's Affiliates, it creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2.1, which grants the Licensee the right to use Licensed Domain Names and Licensed Content in connection with the Business. If the Business is now defined as being operated by the Licensor, it contradicts the license granted to the Licensee. This would make it unclear whether the Licensee can actually use the Licensed Domain Names and Content, since the business is now under the Licensor.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1. Grant of Licenses. (a) Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee, and Licensee hereby accepts from Licensor, an exclusive, non-transferable (except as set forth in Section 10.7) and non-sublicensable (except as provided in Section 2.1(c)) license to use the Licensed Domain Names in connection with the Business during the Term. Except as provided in Section 2.3, Licensee's use of the Licensed Domain Names under the terms of this Agreement shall be free of any fees.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Business' stated that it was operated by the Licensee or its affiliates. The modified definition changes this to the Licensor or its affiliates. This directly contradicts Section 2.1, which grants the Licensee the right to use Licensed Domain Names in connection with the Business. If the Business is now defined as being operated by the Licensor, the Licensee's right to use the domain names is directly undermined, creating a clear and impactful contradiction. This would cause confusion regarding the Licensee's rights and obligations, and could be seen as an attempt to undermine the License granted to the Licensee.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall monitor and censor all Content on the websites associated with the Licensed Domain Names, including without limitation Content posted by end users. Licensor shall also have the right to monitor and censor Content of the websites associated with Licensed Domain Names.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee shall have no obligation to monitor and censor all Content on the websites associated with the Licensed Domain Names, including without limitation Content posted by end users. Licensor shall also have the right to monitor and censor Content of the websites associated with Licensed Domain Names.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the Licensee's obligation to monitor and censor content to 'no obligation', it contradicts the subsequent clause that makes Licensee responsible for removing offending content within a specified timeframe after receiving notice from Licensor or a Governmental Authority. This will create uncertainty about who is primarily responsible for content monitoring and introduces a contradiction with the Licensee's other obligations under this section.
perturbation.location: Section 3.3. Website Monitoring and Censoring. (a) Licensee Obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Section 3.3. Website Monitoring and Censoring. (a) Licensee Obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee shall have no obligation to monitor and censor all Content on the websites associated with the Licensed Domain Names, including without limitation Content posted by end users. Licensor shall also have the right to monitor and censor Content of the websites associated with Licensed Domain Names. Licensee shall remove any offending Content, including, but not limited to, any illegal materials, pornographic, obscene or sexually explicit materials, materials of a violent nature, or politically sensitive materials, from such websites as soon as possible after it becomes aware of such offending Content but in no event later than the timeframe prescribed by the Governmental Authority after receipt of oral or written notice from Licensor or such Governmental Authority. Licensee's failure to comply with this Section 3.3(a) shall be deemed a material breach of this Agreement. Without limiting the foregoing obligations, Licensee acknowledges that Licensor shall have the right to remove such offending Content from the websites associated with Licensed Domain Names.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Licensee has "no obligation" to monitor and censor content. However, the contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text in this case) goes on to say that the Licensee "shall remove any offending Content... as soon as possible after it becomes aware of such offending Content". This creates a contradiction because the Licensee cannot remove offending content if it has no obligation to monitor and find the content in the first place. The change introduces a direct conflict regarding the licensee's responsibility for content moderation, making the text inconsistent and therefore a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The arbitration tribunal shall consist of three (3) arbitrators. The Claimant shall appoint one (1) arbitrator, the Respondent shall appoint one (1) arbitrator, and the two (2) arbitrators so appointed shall appoint a third arbitrator.
perturbation.changed_text: The arbitration tribunal shall consist of one (1) arbitrator. The Claimant shall appoint the arbitrator.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the arbitration tribunal consists of three arbitrators, with each party appointing one and those two selecting a third. Changing this to a single arbitrator appointed solely by the Claimant fundamentally alters the arbitration process and introduces a clear contradiction. This contradicts the initial statement about a three-arbitrator panel and creates an imbalance in the dispute resolution process, potentially influencing the outcome.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) The arbitration tribunal shall consist of one (1) arbitrator. The Claimant shall appoint the arbitrator. If the Claimant and the Respondent fail to appoint one (1) arbitrator, or the two (2) arbitrators appointed fail to appoint the third arbitrator within the time periods set by the then effective Rules, the relevant appointment shall be made promptly by the Commission.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a three-arbitrator panel, while the changed text dictates a single arbitrator appointed by the Claimant. The contradicted text also specifies a one-arbitrator panel appointed by the claimant. However, the explanation states that 'Changing this to a single arbitrator appointed solely by the Claimant fundamentally alters the arbitration process and introduces a clear contradiction. This contradicts the initial statement about a three-arbitrator panel and creates an imbalance in the dispute resolution process, potentially influencing the outcome'. The change is directly contrary to the original panel of three arbitrators mentioned in the original text, resulting in an imbalance in dispute resolution.
